root.title: Uniqueness of Additive Identity
root.text:
Theorem. In a group, the additive identity is unique.

Proof. Suppose e and e' are both additive identities. Since e is an identity, e+e'=e'. Since e' is an identity, e+e'=e. Hence e=e'.

children.text: Both identity laws give e+e'=e' and e+e'=e, hence e=e'.
children.goal: Any two additive identities are equal.
